project.name: barstock
project.description: Barstock (nexio_stock) — single-counter inventory and billing system for a small liquor shop in Odisha, India. Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy 2.0 async + Postgres.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the `app.api` package; contains no logic.

nodes.name: _errors.py
nodes.summary: Shared error-mapping helpers that detect Postgres unique-constraint violations and translate SQLAlchemy/DB integrity errors into consistent FastAPI HTTPException responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Walks exception __cause__/__context__ chains with a seen-id set to avoid infinite loops when classifying wrapped DB errors.

nodes.name: _test_only.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder router mounted only under `app_env == "test"`; kept empty now that its original role-gate test endpoints were superseded by real receiver-only and cashier-only endpoints, but retained so stale test references still import cleanly.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Authentication router providing separate login endpoints for superadmins and shop staff; shares a private `_authenticate` helper that verifies role, shop scope, active status and password before minting a JWT access token.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes superadmin vs shop-scoped login flows behind one shared credential-checking helper to avoid duplicating the password/role/active checks.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: Core FastAPI dependency module: builds DB sessions, resolves the current user from a bearer JWT (401 on missing/invalid/inactive), and exposes `require_role`/`require_shop_scope` guard factories for server-side role enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: require_role is a dependency factory returning a closure (_guard) so routes can declare Depends(require_role(Role.A, Role.B)) with 403 detail listing allowed roles.

nodes.name: health.py
nodes.summary: Liveness and readiness probe endpoints; readiness executes a trivial SQL statement against the DB to confirm connectivity.

nodes.name: shops.py
nodes.summary: Router exposing the caller's own shop record (get/update), restricted to shop-scoped roles, with partial-update semantics that only mutate fields present in the payload and logs which fields changed.

nodes.name: staff.py
nodes.summary: Shop-admin-only router for creating and listing staff accounts within a shop, hashing passwords on creation and mapping unique-constraint violations (duplicate username) to a 409 conflict via `is_unique_violation`.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the insert in try/except around db.commit to catch IntegrityError, rollback, and re-raise as a domain-specific 409 rather than a raw 500.

nodes.name: users.py
nodes.summary: Tiny router exposing a single `GET /me` endpoint that returns the current authenticated user's public profile.

nodes.name: logging_config.py
nodes.summary: Configures structlog + stdlib logging integration (JSON renderer, timestamp/stack-info processors) and silences noisy third-party loggers; exposes `get_logger` for structured module loggers.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges structlog processors into a stdlib logging.StreamHandler via ProcessorFormatter so both structlog and stdlib log calls share one JSON output format.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Application entry point: builds the FastAPI app, wires the async lifespan (DB engine startup/shutdown plus a background low-stock-alert polling task), configures middleware, and mounts every API router.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs `_low_stock_loop` as a long-lived asyncio background task started in `lifespan`, with its own session factory per iteration and cancellation handled via `contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError)` on shutdown.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request/response models for authentication and staff management: login requests (username or phone), token responses, public user representation, and staff/superadmin creation payloads with role and phone-format validators.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic field_validator methods (_phone_format, _only_staff_roles) to enforce phone-number regex shape and restrict creatable roles at the schema layer.

nodes.name: shop.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models for shop data: the public shop representation (GSTIN, excise duty rate, low-stock default) and an update payload that validates GSTIN shape via regex.

nodes.name: jwt.py
nodes.summary: JWT issuance and verification helpers: `create_access_token` embeds subject, shop_id, role and expiry claims; `decode_access_token` validates and decodes, raising a domain-specific `TokenError` on failure.

nodes.name: is_unique_violation
nodes.summary: Walks an exception's __cause__/__context__ chain (guarding against cycles) to detect whether it wraps a Postgres unique-constraint violation.

nodes.name: map_error_to_http
nodes.summary: Maps a caught exception to an appropriate HTTPException using a caller-supplied code-to-status table, falling back to a default status when no mapping matches.

nodes.name: _authenticate
nodes.summary: Shared login helper: looks up a user by shop and identifier field, checks role membership, active status, and password, raising 401 on any failure without leaking which check failed.

nodes.name: login_superadmin
nodes.summary: Login endpoint for superadmin accounts; authenticates by username with no shop scope and returns a signed JWT plus the public user profile.

nodes.name: login_shop
nodes.summary: Login endpoint for shop staff, authenticating within a specific shop_id by username or phone and returning a shop-scoped JWT and profile.

nodes.name: get_current_user
nodes.summary: Resolves the authenticated User from a bearer JWT, raising 401 for missing/invalid tokens or inactive/unknown users, and refreshes the ORM object so attributes are safe to access after the session closes.

nodes.name: require_role
nodes.summary: Dependency factory that returns an inner guard closure enforcing the caller's role is within an allowed set, raising 403 with the permitted-roles list otherwise.

nodes.name: require_shop_scope
nodes.summary: Defensive integrity check ensuring shop-scoped roles always carry a shop_id, raising a 500 if a data integrity violation is detected.

nodes.name: get_my_shop
nodes.summary: Returns the shop record belonging to the current user's shop_id, restricted to shop-scoped roles.

nodes.name: update_my_shop
nodes.summary: Partially updates the caller's own shop, applying only the fields present in the payload, committing, and logging which fields were changed.

nodes.name: create_staff
nodes.summary: Creates a new staff user within the actor's shop, hashing the password, catching unique-constraint violations on commit to return 409 conflict for duplicate usernames, and rolling back on failure.

nodes.name: list_staff
nodes.summary: Lists all staff users belonging to the actor's shop, ordered by id.

nodes.name: configure_logging
nodes.summary: Sets up structlog processors and a stdlib logging handler sharing a JSON formatter, sets the root log level, and quiets known noisy third-party loggers (e.g. uvicorn access logs).

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: FastAPI lifespan context manager: configures logging, initializes the DB engine, starts the background low-stock-polling task, and cancels/awaits it cleanly on shutdown.

nodes.name: _low_stock_loop
nodes.summary: Long-running background loop that periodically wakes up, opens a fresh DB session per shop, computes low-stock alerts, and logs results, catching and logging errors per iteration so the loop survives transient failures.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs as an asyncio task for the app's lifetime; opens its own session per iteration rather than sharing a request-scoped session, since it runs outside any HTTP request context.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Application factory that instantiates FastAPI with the lifespan, adds middleware, and registers every API router (auth, checkout, dashboard, health, lots, products, shops, staff, users, voids).

nodes.name: LoginRequest
nodes.summary: Login request payload accepting either username or phone plus password, with a validator enforcing phone-number format.

nodes.name: TokenResponse
nodes.summary: Response model for successful login carrying the access token, token type, expiry, and embedded public user profile.

nodes.name: UserPublic
nodes.summary: Public-facing representation of a user record, excluding sensitive fields like password hash.

nodes.name: StaffCreate
nodes.summary: Payload for creating a staff account, validated to restrict the role field to permitted staff roles and enforce phone-number format.

nodes.name: SuperAdminCreate
nodes.summary: Minimal payload for creating a superadmin account with just username and password.

nodes.name: ShopPublic
nodes.summary: Public representation of a shop including GSTIN, excise duty rate, and default low-stock threshold.

nodes.name: ShopUpdate
nodes.summary: Partial-update payload for shop settings with a validator enforcing GSTIN shape via regex.

nodes.name: TokenError
nodes.summary: Domain-specific exception raised when JWT decoding or validation fails.

nodes.name: create_access_token
nodes.summary: Builds and signs a JWT embedding subject, shop_id, role, extra claims, and issued-at/expiry timestamps based on configured TTL.

nodes.name: decode_access_token
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates a JWT using the configured secret, raising TokenError with the underlying reason on failure.

nodes.name: lots.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router for stock receiving: creates lots (stock-in batches) with per-barcode line validation against the receiving shop, lists lots with pagination, and fetches a single lot by id, writing a StockinLog audit entry on every create.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLAlchemy async select/scalars patterns and a private _load_lines helper to eagerly populate lot line relationships before serialization.

nodes.name: create_lot
nodes.summary: Validates that all barcodes in the incoming lot payload exist and are active for the receiver's shop, then creates the Lot and its LotLine rows in one transaction and writes a StockinLog entry before returning the hydrated LotPublic response.

nodes.name: list_lots
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated, shop-scoped list of lots ordered by most recently received.

nodes.name: get_lot
nodes.summary: Fetches a single lot by id scoped to the actor's shop, raising 404 if not found or not owned by the shop.

nodes.name: _load_lines
nodes.summary: Internal helper that refreshes a Lot's lines relationship (with nested product) so the response can be serialized without additional lazy-load queries.

nodes.name: products.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router for product catalog management: create/list/lookup/update single products, plus a bulk CSV import endpoint that parses, validates, and upserts many rows per request with per-row error collection.
nodes.languageNotes: import_products_csv alone spans ~145 lines with nested per-row validation, decimal/int parsing, uniqueness handling via savepoint-style flush+rollback, and structured per-row error reporting -- by far the most complex function in this batch.

nodes.name: create_product
nodes.summary: Creates a single product for the actor's shop, catching unique-barcode constraint violations and translating them into a 409 HTTP response.

nodes.name: list_products
nodes.summary: Lists products for the actor's shop with optional active-only filtering, brand search, and pagination.

nodes.name: lookup_product
nodes.summary: Looks up a single active product by barcode within the actor's shop, used by the checkout scanning flow.

nodes.name: update_product
nodes.summary: Applies a partial update to a product's mutable fields (brand, size, price, threshold, active flag) and logs which fields changed.

nodes.name: import_products_csv
nodes.summary: Bulk-imports products from an uploaded CSV file: decodes with UTF-8/UTF-8-BOM fallback, validates required headers, then for each row parses and validates barcode/brand/size/price/threshold, inserts the product, and on a per-row unique-constraint violation rolls back just that row's savepoint-like flush and records a structured ProductImportError instead of aborting the whole import.
nodes.languageNotes: Row-level flush+rollback isolation lets one bad row fail without discarding previously-validated rows in the same request -- a manual poor-man's savepoint pattern since SQLAlchemy async doesn't expose nested transactions as simply as sync.

nodes.name: log.py
nodes.summary: Defines the shared audit-log ORM base (_BaseLog) and three concrete log tables -- InvoicingLog, StockinLog, AdminLog -- each recording shop, actor, event type, and a JSON payload for traceability of billing, stock-receiving, and admin actions.

nodes.name: _BaseLog
nodes.summary: Abstract base declaring the common audit-log columns (id, shop_id, actor_user_id, event_type, payload, created_at) shared by all concrete log tables.

nodes.name: InvoicingLog
nodes.summary: Audit log table recording billing/invoicing-related events (finalize, void, reversal) with shop and actor relationships.

nodes.name: StockinLog
nodes.summary: Audit log table recording stock-receiving (lot creation) events with shop and actor relationships.

nodes.name: AdminLog
nodes.summary: Audit log table recording administrative actions with shop and actor relationships.

nodes.name: lot.py
nodes.summary: ORM models for stock-receiving: Lot (a single receiving event with reference/notes) and LotLine (per-product quantity received within a lot), forming the source-of-truth for derivable stock-on-hand.

nodes.name: Lot
nodes.summary: Represents one stock-receiving event for a shop, with reference/notes metadata and a one-to-many relationship to LotLine rows.

nodes.name: LotLine
nodes.summary: Represents the quantity of one product received within a specific lot; summed across lots minus sales this derives current stock.

nodes.name: product.py
nodes.summary: ORM model for the Product entity: barcode, brand, size label, price, low-stock threshold, active flag, and shop scoping -- the core catalog record referenced by lots, invoices, and low-stock computation.

nodes.name: Product
nodes.summary: Core catalog entity with barcode uniqueness, pricing, per-product low-stock threshold override, and an is_active soft-delete flag.

nodes.name: lot.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request/response schemas for the lots API: LotLineCreate/LotCreate for stock-in payloads (with a validator rejecting duplicate barcodes within one lot) and LotPublic/LotListResponse for serialized output.

nodes.name: LotCreate
nodes.summary: Request schema for creating a lot; validates that barcodes are not duplicated within the same submitted lot via a field validator.

nodes.name: LotPublic
nodes.summary: Serialized response schema representing a lot and its lines returned to API clients.

nodes.name: product.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas for the products API covering create/update/public representations plus CSV-import-specific row, error, and response schemas, with barcode and size-label format validators shared across create and import paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Regex-based field validators (_barcode_no_ws, _size_label_shape) are duplicated between ProductCreate and ProductImportRow rather than factored into a shared mixin/validator.

nodes.name: ProductCreate
nodes.summary: Request schema for creating a product, enforcing barcode whitespace rules and size-label shape via field validators.

nodes.name: ProductImportRow
nodes.summary: Schema representing one validated row parsed from an uploaded product CSV, reusing barcode format validation.

nodes.name: ProductImportResponse
nodes.summary: Response schema summarizing a CSV import run: counts of created/failed rows plus the list of per-row errors.

nodes.name: low_stock.py
nodes.summary: Computes low-stock alerts per shop by deriving current stock as received-minus-sold via a UNION ALL of two aggregated subqueries, then comparing against each product's effective threshold (per-product override falling back to shop default) and returning rows sorted by most urgent first.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a full-outer-join-style UNION ALL of two subqueries (received via LotLine, sold via InvoiceLine filtered to counted statuses) to compute stock from two independent tables without a stock ledger table -- a non-trivial derived-stock computation.

nodes.name: _stock_per_product
nodes.summary: Builds and executes a combined received/sold aggregation query (via UNION ALL of two subqueries with an outer join fallback) to derive net current stock per active product in a shop.

nodes.name: compute_low_stock
nodes.summary: Loads all active products for a shop, computes their derived stock, compares against the effective (per-product or shop-default) low-stock threshold, and returns matching rows sorted by most urgent (lowest stock relative to threshold) first.

nodes.name: LowStockRow
nodes.summary: Simple data-carrier representing one low-stock alert row: product, current stock, and the threshold that was breached.

nodes.name: test_checkout.py
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the checkout/invoicing flow covering single- and multi-line finalize, split payments, payment-mismatch rejection, stock-exceeding and unknown-barcode rejection, idempotency-key replay and enforcement, concurrent finalize race safety for the last unit of stock, role-based access control, invoice line immutability, invoicing-log writes, and PDF receipt generation.
nodes.languageNotes: test_two_concurrent_finalizes_for_last_unit_only_one_wins spins up two real concurrent HTTP calls via asyncio.gather against separate httpx AsyncClient/ASGITransport instances and a fresh DB session to verify optimistic-locking / row-level contention behavior -- a genuine concurrency test, not just a unit test.

nodes.name: test_two_concurrent_finalizes_for_last_unit_only_one_wins
nodes.summary: Fires two concurrent checkout finalize requests for the same last unit of stock via asyncio.gather and asserts exactly one succeeds while the other is rejected, verifying the system prevents overselling under race conditions.

nodes.name: test_idempotency_key_replay_returns_same_invoice
nodes.summary: Verifies that replaying a checkout request with the same idempotency key returns the original invoice rather than creating a duplicate.

nodes.name: test_finalized_invoice_lines_are_immutable
nodes.summary: Asserts that once an invoice is finalized, its line items cannot be altered by subsequent requests.

nodes.name: test_invoice_pdf_is_a_real_pdf
nodes.summary: Finalizes an invoice and fetches its PDF receipt, asserting the response body starts with the PDF file signature bytes.

nodes.name: test_lots.py
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the lots (stock-receiving) API covering single- and multi-line lot creation, role-based access control (receiver/owner allowed, cashier rejected), unknown-barcode and cross-shop-barcode rejection, duplicate-barcode-in-lot rejection, stockin-log writes, derivable stock increase after receiving, and shop-scoped lot listing/retrieval.

nodes.name: test_receiving_increases_derivable_stock
nodes.summary: Receives stock for two products and asserts that summed LotLine quantities match the amounts received, verifying stock is correctly derivable from lot lines.

nodes.name: test_lot_listing_is_shop_scoped
nodes.summary: Verifies that lot listing only returns lots belonging to the actor's own shop.

nodes.name: test_low_stock.py
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for the low-stock alert feature: no-alert baseline, per-product threshold overrides, at-threshold boundary behavior, stock reduction from sales triggering alerts, override-beats-shop-default precedence, most-urgent-first sort order, receiver visibility, and exclusion of inactive products.

nodes.name: test_per_product_override_beats_shop_default
nodes.summary: Sets both a shop-wide default threshold and a conflicting per-product override, and asserts the per-product override takes precedence in low-stock computation.

nodes.name: test_sale_brings_product_below_threshold
nodes.summary: Finalizes a sale that reduces stock below the configured threshold and asserts the product then appears in the low-stock alert list.

nodes.name: test_voids.py
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for invoice voiding and reversal: direct cashier void pre-EOD with stock restoration, rejecting double-voids, invoicing-log writes on void, owner-approval workflow for post-signoff void requests (pending/approve/reject), and role-based restrictions on who can approve.

nodes.name: test_owner_approving_pending_void_creates_reversal
nodes.summary: Verifies that an owner approving a pending void request on a signed-off invoice creates a reversal record rather than mutating the original invoice.

nodes.name: test_pre_eod_void_restores_stock
nodes.summary: Voids an invoice before end-of-day signoff and asserts the previously-deducted stock quantities are restored.

nodes.name: _mark_signed_off
nodes.summary: Test helper that directly flips an invoice's status to signed-off in the DB session to set up post-EOD void-approval scenarios.

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Alembic migration environment script that configures both offline (SQL-emit-only) and online (live async connection) migration execution against the app's async SQLAlchemy engine and Base metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges Alembic's synchronous migration context with the app's async engine via connection.run_sync inside an async wrapper.

nodes.name: run_migrations_offline
nodes.summary: Configures Alembic to emit migration SQL against a URL only, without an active DB connection, for offline/dry-run migration generation.

nodes.name: do_run_migrations
nodes.summary: Configures the Alembic context on a live synchronous connection and runs migrations within a transaction.

nodes.name: run_async_migrations
nodes.summary: Creates an async engine from the Alembic config section and runs migrations on it via run_sync, then disposes the engine.

nodes.name: run_migrations_online
nodes.summary: Entry point that drives the async migration path via asyncio.run.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker file for the app package with no logic.

nodes.name: cli.py
nodes.summary: Command-line entry point for shop bootstrap operations: creates the initial superadmin account and provisions new shops with their owner user, using async DB transactions with duplicate checks and rollback on failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes argv parsing, interactive prompts (getpass/input), and asyncio.run-driven DB transactions in a single dispatcher.

nodes.name: _createsuperadmin
nodes.summary: Async CLI operation that validates username/password length, ensures no duplicate superadmin exists, and inserts a new superadmin user with a bcrypt-hashed password.

nodes.name: _createshop
nodes.summary: Async CLI operation that creates a new shop and its owner user in one transaction, rolling back and exiting with an error if the shop code or owner username is already taken.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI argument dispatcher that parses argv for create-superadmin/create-shop subcommands, prompting interactively for missing credentials before invoking the corresponding async operation.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-settings based application configuration (env vars, DB URL, JWT/bcrypt parameters, CORS, low-stock check interval) exposed via a cached get_settings() accessor.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic settings model declaring all environment-driven configuration: app metadata, logging, JWT/bcrypt security parameters, database URL, CORS origins, and low-stock alert interval.

nodes.name: get_settings
nodes.summary: LRU-cached factory returning a singleton Settings instance so configuration is parsed from the environment only once.

nodes.name: db.py
nodes.summary: Async SQLAlchemy engine/session management: builds and caches the async engine and sessionmaker from settings, provides a FastAPI dependency (get_db) with automatic rollback on error, and an init_db() helper to create tables from metadata.

nodes.name: Base
nodes.summary: Declarative base class from which all SQLAlchemy ORM models in the project inherit.

nodes.name: get_engine
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and caches a module-level async SQLAlchemy engine configured from the settings' database_url.

nodes.name: get_sessionmaker
nodes.summary: Builds an async_sessionmaker bound to the cached engine for creating new database sessions.

nodes.name: reset_engine
nodes.summary: Disposes and clears the cached engine/sessionmaker, used to force reconnection, e.g. between isolated test runs.

nodes.name: get_db
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency that yields an async DB session per request, exiting cleanly on success and rolling back automatically on exception.

nodes.name: init_db
nodes.summary: Creates all ORM tables from Base metadata against the async engine; used for test setup and initial bootstrap.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting all ORM model classes (invoice, log, lot, product, shop, user) so Alembic and application code can import them from a single location.

nodes.name: shop.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM model for the Shop entity, holding shop identity, low-stock defaults, invoice numbering counter, GSTIN and excise duty rate, plus the relationship to its Users.

nodes.name: Shop
nodes.summary: ORM model for a shop's identity and configuration: name, unique code, low-stock threshold default, running invoice number counter, GSTIN, and excise duty rate, with a one-to-many relationship to Users.

nodes.name: user.py
nodes.summary: Defines the UserRole enum (superadmin/owner/receiver/cashier) and the User ORM model carrying credentials, role, and shop association used for authentication and authorization.

nodes.name: UserRole
nodes.summary: Enum of the four user roles in the system (superadmin, owner, receiver, cashier) used to drive role-based access control across the app.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: ORM model for shop staff accounts: credentials, role, contact info, and shop association, with a many-to-one relationship back to Shop.

nodes.name: passwords.py
nodes.summary: Thin bcrypt wrapper providing hash_password and verify_password helpers used by authentication and user-provisioning code.

nodes.name: hash_password
nodes.summary: Hashes a plaintext password with bcrypt using a configurable cost factor from settings, truncating input to bcrypt's 72-byte limit.

nodes.name: verify_password
nodes.summary: Verifies a plaintext password against a stored bcrypt hash.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest fixture suite provisioning an isolated per-test Postgres database (via an asyncpg admin connection plus an Alembic subprocess migration run), plus DB session, ASGI test client, and pre-seeded shop/owner/receiver/cashier/superadmin fixtures with logged-in HTTP clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.to_thread to run blocking Alembic subprocess migrations from an async fixture, and truncates tables between tests via TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY CASCADE for isolation.

nodes.name: test_db_dsn
nodes.summary: Session-scoped fixture that provisions an isolated, uniquely-named test Postgres database via an asyncpg admin connection, runs Alembic migrations against it in a background thread, yields its DSN, then drops the database on teardown.

nodes.name: _truncate_tables
nodes.summary: Truncates all application tables with identity restart and cascade between tests to reset state without re-running migrations.

nodes.name: db_session
nodes.summary: Function-scoped fixture providing a fresh async DB session bound to the isolated test database, truncating tables before each test.

nodes.name: client
nodes.summary: Function-scoped fixture providing an httpx AsyncClient wired to the FastAPI app via ASGITransport against the isolated test database, resetting the engine and settings cache on teardown.

nodes.name: _make_shop
nodes.summary: Helper that inserts and commits a Shop row for use by higher-level fixtures.

nodes.name: _make_user
nodes.summary: Helper that hashes a password and inserts a User row with the given role/shop association for use by higher-level fixtures.

nodes.name: shop
nodes.summary: Fixture producing a default seeded Shop for tests.

nodes.name: owner
nodes.summary: Fixture producing a seeded owner-role User for the test shop.

nodes.name: receiver
nodes.summary: Fixture producing a seeded receiver-role User for the test shop.

nodes.name: cashier
nodes.summary: Fixture producing a seeded cashier-role User for the test shop.

nodes.name: superadmin
nodes.summary: Fixture producing a seeded superadmin-role User not tied to any shop.

nodes.name: _make_logged_in_client
nodes.summary: Helper that logs a given user in via the login endpoint and returns an AsyncClient with the resulting auth token attached.

nodes.name: owner_client
nodes.summary: Fixture yielding an authenticated HTTP client for the owner role.

nodes.name: receiver_client
nodes.summary: Fixture yielding an authenticated HTTP client for the receiver role.

nodes.name: cashier_client
nodes.summary: Fixture yielding an authenticated HTTP client for the cashier role.

nodes.name: superadmin_client
nodes.summary: Fixture yielding an authenticated HTTP client for the superadmin role.

nodes.name: test_shops.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for shop settings (GSTIN, excise duty rate) covering role-based update permissions, input validation, and verifying GST/duty values and shop name propagate correctly into generated invoice PDFs.

nodes.name: _seed_product
nodes.summary: Helper posting a new product with the given barcode via the API.

nodes.name: _seed_lot
nodes.summary: Helper receiving stock into a lot for a given barcode/quantity via the receiver API.

nodes.name: _finalize
nodes.summary: Helper that finalizes a cashier sale for a barcode/quantity/amount and returns the parsed invoice response.

nodes.name: test_get_shop_returns_defaults
nodes.summary: Verifies the shop settings endpoint returns the DB-seeded default values for a fresh shop.

nodes.name: test_owner_updates_gstin_and_duty_rate
nodes.summary: Verifies an owner can update GSTIN and excise duty rate via PATCH and that the change persists in the database.

nodes.name: test_cashier_cannot_update_shop
nodes.summary: Verifies a cashier is forbidden from updating shop settings, enforcing role-based access control.

nodes.name: test_invalid_gstin_rejected
nodes.summary: Verifies malformed GSTIN values are rejected by input validation.

nodes.name: test_duty_rate_must_be_0_to_100
nodes.summary: Verifies the excise duty rate is constrained to the 0-100 range.

nodes.name: test_pdf_includes_configured_gstin
nodes.summary: Verifies a configured GSTIN appears in the generated invoice PDF text after a full seed-lot-finalize flow.

nodes.name: test_pdf_includes_configured_duty_rate
nodes.summary: Verifies a configured excise duty rate appears in the generated invoice PDF text.

nodes.name: test_pdf_includes_placeholder_disclaimer
nodes.summary: Verifies the invoice PDF includes a placeholder/disclaimer notice for unconfigured tax fields.

nodes.name: test_pdf_does_not_hardcode_cgst_or_sgst
nodes.summary: Verifies the invoice PDF does not hardcode CGST/SGST tax labels, using regex checks against the rendered text.

nodes.name: test_shop_name_appears_on_invoice
nodes.summary: Verifies the configured shop name is rendered on the generated invoice PDF.

nodes.name: checkout.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI router for the checkout flow: finalizes a cart through an idempotent POST, and fetches a finalized invoice or its rendered PDF.

nodes.name: dashboard.py
nodes.summary: Owner-facing dashboard API exposing EOD sign-off, EOD totals/history, the pending void queue, and low-stock alerts as read/write endpoints.

nodes.name: voids.py
nodes.summary: API endpoints for the two-step void workflow: cashier-initiated void/void-request, and owner approve/reject of post-EOD void requests, plus an audit-log writer shared by all three.

nodes.name: invoice.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM models for the sales domain: Invoice, InvoiceLine, Payment, EodSignOff, and IdempotencyKey, plus the InvoiceStatus/PaymentMode enums that drive the void/reversal state machine.

nodes.name: checkout.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request/response schemas for the checkout API: cart line and payment inputs with a payments-sum validator, and invoice/payment public projections.

nodes.name: eod.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic response schemas for the owner dashboard: EOD sign-off, EOD totals, sign-off history, pending void queue, and low-stock alerts.

nodes.name: finalize
nodes.summary: Checkout finalize endpoint: builds cart/payment lines from the request, calls the checkout service, maps domain errors to HTTP responses, records an invoicing log entry, and returns the finalized invoice or replay flag.

nodes.name: _load_invoice_or_404
nodes.summary: Loads an invoice with its lines and payments eagerly, raising a 404 if it does not exist.

nodes.name: get_invoice
nodes.summary: Read-only endpoint returning a finalized invoice by id.

nodes.name: get_invoice_pdf
nodes.summary: Streams a rendered PDF for a given invoice, loading the owning shop's GST/excise fields before handing off to the PDF renderer.

nodes.name: sign_off
nodes.summary: Owner endpoint to sign off a business day; delegates to the EOD service and maps domain errors such as double sign-off or future-date rejection to HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: eod_totals
nodes.summary: Returns aggregated revenue and payment totals for a business date, sorted by payment mode.

nodes.name: eod_history
nodes.summary: Lists past sign-offs within an optional date range, most recent first.

nodes.name: void_queue
nodes.summary: Lists invoices currently pending owner approval for post-EOD void, refreshing relationships before serialization.

nodes.name: low_stock
nodes.summary: Computes and returns products below their effective stock threshold for owner attention.

nodes.name: request_void
nodes.summary: Cashier-facing void endpoint: routes to a same-day direct void or a post-EOD void request depending on invoice state, commits, writes an audit-log entry, and returns the updated invoice.

nodes.name: approve_void
nodes.summary: Owner endpoint approving a pending post-EOD void, creating the reversal invoice via the service layer and writing an audit-log entry.

nodes.name: reject_void
nodes.summary: Owner endpoint rejecting a pending post-EOD void request, restoring the invoice to its prior status and logging the decision.

nodes.name: _load_invoice
nodes.summary: Loads an invoice scoped to a shop, raising 404 if it is missing or out of scope.

nodes.name: _write_void_log
nodes.summary: Writes a structured InvoicingLog audit entry capturing a void/reversal state transition, the acting user, reason, and extra context.

nodes.name: InvoiceStatus
nodes.summary: Enum of invoice lifecycle states (finalized, pending void, voided, reversed, etc.) that drives the void/reversal state machine.

nodes.name: PaymentMode
nodes.summary: Enum of accepted payment modes (e.g. cash, card, UPI) used on Payment records.

nodes.name: Invoice
nodes.summary: Core sale record: cashier, status, total amount, EOD sign-off fields, void-request metadata, and a self-referencing link to the invoice it reverses.

nodes.name: InvoiceLine
nodes.summary: Line item on an invoice: product, quantity, unit price, and computed line total.

nodes.name: Payment
nodes.summary: A payment applied to an invoice, capturing mode and amount to support split payments.

nodes.name: EodSignOff
nodes.summary: Record of a business day's end-of-day sign-off: who signed off, when, and how many invoices were included.

nodes.name: IdempotencyKey
nodes.summary: Persisted idempotency key per shop mapping a client-supplied key to the invoice it produced, enabling safe checkout retries.

nodes.name: CheckoutFinalizeRequest
nodes.summary: Checkout request payload of cart lines and payments, with a validator rejecting non-positive total payment amounts.

nodes.name: InvoicePublic
nodes.summary: Public projection of an Invoice including its lines and payments, returned by the checkout and invoice endpoints.

nodes.name: CheckoutFinalizeResponse
nodes.summary: Wraps the finalized invoice plus an is_replay flag signaling an idempotent replay of a prior request.

nodes.name: SignOffRequest
nodes.summary: Sign-off request payload with a validator rejecting business dates too far in the past.

nodes.name: SignOffResponse
nodes.summary: Response confirming a completed sign-off with signer, timestamp, and invoice count.

nodes.name: EodTotalsResponse
nodes.summary: Aggregated totals for a business date: revenue, invoice/void/reversal counts, and a per-payment-mode breakdown.

nodes.name: SignOffHistoryResponse
nodes.summary: Wraps a list of historical sign-off records.

nodes.name: PendingVoidResponse
nodes.summary: Wraps a list of invoices awaiting an owner void decision.

nodes.name: LowStockResponse
nodes.summary: Wraps a list of low-stock items with an evaluation timestamp.

nodes.name: checkout.py
nodes.summary: Core checkout engine: resolves cart barcodes to products, computes current stock via received-minus-sold subqueries, and atomically finalizes a sale under a row lock (FOR UPDATE) with idempotency-key replay and payment-split validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLAlchemy `with_for_update()` row locks on Product and Shop rows plus a UNION ALL subquery pattern to compute net stock, making this one of the most concurrency-sensitive files in the codebase.

nodes.name: eod.py
nodes.summary: End-of-day reconciliation service: enforces once-per-business-day sign-off, aggregates a day's invoice and payment totals excluding voids/reversals, and lists sign-off history and pending post-EOD void requests.

nodes.name: invoice_pdf.py
nodes.summary: Renders a finalized invoice as a PDF using ReportLab, building a document story with header, line-item table, payment table, and totals/footer.

nodes.name: voids.py
nodes.summary: Two-tier void/reversal state machine: same-day direct voids apply immediately, while post-EOD voids require an owner request/approve/reject cycle where approval creates a signed reversal invoice under a row lock.
nodes.languageNotes: approve_post_eod_void row-locks the Shop and constructs a full mirrored reversal Invoice (negated lines and payments), making it the most state-sensitive function in this file.

nodes.name: test_eod.py
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for EOD sign-off, EOD totals/history, and pending-void-queue endpoints, using seeded products/lots and a shared checkout helper to set up invoice state.

nodes.name: CheckoutError
nodes.summary: Domain exception for checkout failures, carrying an error code and optional field/reason context used to map to HTTP responses.

nodes.name: _check_idempotency_key_present
nodes.summary: Validates that a required idempotency key header is present and non-empty, raising CheckoutError otherwise.

nodes.name: _is_today_signed_off
nodes.summary: Checks whether today's business date already has an EOD sign-off recorded for the shop, which blocks further checkouts.

nodes.name: _resolve_products_for_cart
nodes.summary: Resolves cart barcodes to active Product rows for a shop, raising CheckoutError listing any barcodes that fail to match.

nodes.name: _current_stock_for
nodes.summary: Computes current on-hand stock per product via a UNION of received-quantity and sold-quantity subqueries (received lots minus quantities on finalized invoice lines).
nodes.languageNotes: Builds a hand-rolled outer-join + UNION ALL SQLAlchemy Core query to net received lot quantities against sold invoice quantities per product, including products with sales but no recorded receipts.

nodes.name: finalize_checkout
nodes.summary: Atomically finalizes a sale: validates the idempotency key, signed-off-day state, and payment totals; replays an existing idempotency key if one is found; row-locks the affected products and shop (FOR UPDATE); verifies sufficient stock; and creates the Invoice, InvoiceLine, Payment, and IdempotencyKey rows within one transaction.
nodes.languageNotes: The single most sensitive function in the codebase: combines idempotent replay, multi-row FOR UPDATE locking, and Decimal-precise money math in one 170+ line transaction.

nodes.name: EodError
nodes.summary: Domain exception for EOD sign-off failures, carrying an error code used to map to HTTP responses.

nodes.name: EodTotals
nodes.summary: Value object carrying a business date's aggregated totals (revenue, counts, per-mode payments) as returned by get_day_totals.

nodes.name: sign_off_day
nodes.summary: Signs off a business day: rejects future dates and duplicate sign-offs, marks all of the day's finalized invoices as signed off, and records an EodSignOff row.

nodes.name: get_day_totals
nodes.summary: Aggregates a business date's invoice counts (finalized/voided/reversed), revenue, and per-payment-mode totals, whether or not the day has been signed off.

nodes.name: list_signoff_history
nodes.summary: Lists EodSignOff records for a shop, optionally filtered by date range, most recent first.

nodes.name: list_pending_voids
nodes.summary: Lists invoices currently awaiting owner void approval, oldest request first.

nodes.name: render_invoice_pdf
nodes.summary: Builds a printable invoice PDF: header with shop/GST details, a line-item table, a payment breakdown table, and a totals/footer section, returned as bytes.

nodes.name: VoidError
nodes.summary: Domain exception for void/reversal failures, carrying an error code used to map to HTTP responses.

nodes.name: VoidResult
nodes.summary: Value object pairing the voided invoice with its reversal invoice, when one was created.

nodes.name: _load_invoice_for_void
nodes.summary: Loads an invoice scoped to a shop for void processing, raising VoidError if it is not found.

nodes.name: direct_void
nodes.summary: Same-day void path: validates the invoice is finalized and not already signed off, then marks it VOIDED with actor, reason, and timestamp.

nodes.name: request_post_eod_void
nodes.summary: Cashier requests a void for an invoice already past EOD sign-off, moving it to PENDING_VOID with a recorded reason and timestamp for owner review.

nodes.name: approve_post_eod_void
nodes.summary: Owner approves a pending post-EOD void: row-locks the shop, creates a mirrored reversal Invoice with negated line items and payments, and marks the original invoice VOIDED.
nodes.languageNotes: Row-locks the Shop with FOR UPDATE and builds a full mirror-image reversal Invoice (negated InvoiceLine and Payment rows) in the same transaction as the status change.

nodes.name: reject_post_eod_void
nodes.summary: Owner rejects a pending post-EOD void request, restoring the invoice to FINALIZED with a rejection reason recorded.

nodes.name: _finalize
nodes.summary: Test helper that posts a checkout with a generated idempotency key and returns the parsed invoice response.

nodes.name: test_owner_signs_off_today
nodes.summary: Verifies an owner can sign off today's business date after finalized sales exist.

nodes.name: test_double_sign_off_returns_409
nodes.summary: Verifies signing off an already-signed-off day returns a 409 conflict.

nodes.name: test_checkout_rejected_after_eod_sign_off
nodes.summary: Verifies checkout is rejected once the business day has been signed off.

nodes.name: test_eod_totals_for_signed_off_day
nodes.summary: Verifies EOD totals correctly aggregate revenue for a signed-off day with multiple sales.

nodes.name: test_eod_totals_for_unsigned_day_returns_zero_signed_off_false
nodes.summary: Verifies EOD totals report signed_off=false and still reflect sales even before sign-off.

nodes.name: test_eod_totals_excludes_voided_and_reversal_from_revenue
nodes.summary: Verifies voided and reversal invoices are excluded from EOD revenue totals.

nodes.name: test_eod_history_lists_signoffs_in_descending_order
nodes.summary: Verifies sign-off history is returned most-recent-first across two signed-off days.

nodes.name: test_eod_history_filters_by_date_range
nodes.summary: Verifies sign-off history filtering by from/to date range excludes out-of-range sign-offs.

nodes.name: test_void_queue_lists_pending_invoices
nodes.summary: Verifies the pending void queue lists an invoice after a cashier submits a post-EOD void request.

nodes.name: test_void_queue_excludes_resolved_voids
nodes.summary: Verifies the pending void queue no longer lists an invoice once its void request has been approved.

nodes.name: test_eod_signoff_record_persists
nodes.summary: Verifies a sign-off persists exactly one EodSignOff row in the database.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file listing the app, JWT/auth, database, and CORS settings a developer must copy to `.env` — includes the dev SECRET_KEY, bcrypt cost, JWT access-token TTL tuned for a single shift (12h), and the asyncpg DATABASE_URL shared by the app and Alembic.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for the Barstock liquor-shop inventory/billing system: documents the FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/Postgres stack, a 5-step local quickstart (uv sync, docker compose up, env copy + migrations + superuser, run uvicorn, open /docs), the pytest/ruff test workflow, and the app/ directory layout.

nodes.name: alembic.ini
nodes.summary: Standard Alembic migration-runner configuration pointing script_location at the alembic/ directory (relative to the ini file via %(here)s) with default logging and revision-naming settings largely unmodified from the Alembic template.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Defines a single local-dev/test Postgres 16 (alpine) service named barstock-db with barstock/barstock credentials, port 5432 exposed, a named volume for data persistence, and a pg_isready healthcheck.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Project metadata and build config (hatchling) declaring FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/Alembic/Pydantic/structlog/passlib runtime dependencies plus a pytest/ruff dev extra, the `barstock` CLI entry point (app.cli:main), extensive ruff lint rule selection with justified global and per-file ignores (tests, app/cli.py, app/db.py, app/models/user.py, app/models/invoice.py, app/schemas/auth.py, alembic/), and pytest/coverage settings (asyncio auto mode, strict markers, coverage source=app excluding cli.py).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ruff's per-file-ignores table extensively with inline comments justifying each suppressed rule (e.g. StrEnum pattern for FastAPI-friendly enums, intentional broad excepts in cleanup paths) — a notable documentation-as-config pattern.

nodes.name: 00-intake.md
nodes.summary: Initial project intake for Barstock: restates the raw wishlist (barcode-based product onboarding, checkout/invoicing, cash/UPI payment recording, EOD reconciliation), clarifies that 'punch in/punch out' means stock check-in/check-out rather than staff attendance, lists open unknowns (users, scale, compliance, stack), and surveys existing commercial liquor-POS competitors (VasyERP, Kamiti, Romio) before closing Phase 0 confirmation.

nodes.name: 01-branch-map.md
nodes.summary: Fans the confirmed intake out into 14 architectural domains (users/personas, domain model, backend/API, frontend/UX, data/persistence, auth/security, integrations, observability, failure modes, testing, performance, deployment, compliance, docs) each with implications and open questions, plus a dependency-ordered grilling sequence for Phase 2.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured as 17 numbered/lettered sections with per-domain open-question lists (Q1.1-Q14.2), the most structurally elaborate planning document besides the PRD.

nodes.name: 02-ledger.md
nodes.summary: Chronological decisions ledger recording ~63 numbered decisions (D-1 through D-63) made across Phase 0 confirmation, Phase 2 grilling, a logging-detail follow-up, and the Phase 4 absence hunt — covering roles, domain model (Lot/Product/Invoice), concurrency (multi-counter selling), payment modes, void/reversal workflow, storage tiers, and compliance scoping for Odisha excise/GST.

nodes.name: 03-prd.md
nodes.summary: The frozen Product Requirements Document for Barstock: problem statement, goals, non-goals, and 44 numbered requirements (R-1 through R-44) organized by the 14 branch-map domains, plus a roles summary table, a deferred-items table with v1 defaults, and a verbatim appendix reproducing the full decisions ledger. This is the authoritative, re-frozen spec that all implementation work traces back to.
nodes.languageNotes: The longest and most structurally dense document in the harness (27 headings across 3 nesting levels), because it both defines requirements and embeds the entire ledger as an appendix.

nodes.name: 04-absences.md
nodes.summary: Short Phase 4 'absence hunt' report listing 14 gaps never discussed against the frozen PRD and ledger (split payments, partial returns, bulk catalog import, printed receipts, EOD scope across counters, cost/margin tracking, discounts, duplicate barcodes, password reset, backups, day-boundary, concurrent sessions, trend reporting, multi-shop provisioning), with a disposition noting all findings were resolved into ledger entries D-50 through D-63 and folded back into the PRD.

nodes.name: README
nodes.summary: Boilerplate one-line Alembic README (generic 'Generic single-database configuration' text auto-created by `alembic init`).

nodes.name: script.py.mako
nodes.summary: Alembic migration script template used to scaffold new revision files with upgrade/downgrade stubs and revision metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Mako templating syntax generates boilerplate Python migration files consumed by `alembic revision`.

nodes.name: 0243c7acf544_foundation_shops_users_log_tables.py
nodes.summary: Foundation migration (slice #1) creating the core schema: superadmins, shops, shop_users (with role enum), plus the invoicing_logs, stockin_logs, and admin_logs audit tables with indexes and unique constraints.

nodes.name: 058759837183_eod_eod_signed_off_at_eod_signoffs_table.py
nodes.summary: EOD reconciliation migration (slice #6) that creates the eod_signoffs table and adds a signed_off_at / signed_off_by foreign key to track owner sign-off on daily reconciliation.

nodes.name: 2e68e776867b_voids_void_reversal_pending_void_status_.py
nodes.summary: Void & reversal migration (slice #5) adding reversal_of_invoice_id, voided_by, and voided_at columns plus an index/foreign keys to support owner-approved invoice voids and pending-void status tracking.

nodes.name: 3efd6817d954_lots_lot_lot_lines_tables.py
nodes.summary: Lot receiving migration (slice #3) creating the lots and lot_lines tables that record stock check-in batches and per-product quantities received.

nodes.name: 423948112aae_products_catalog_shop_low_stock_.py
nodes.summary: Product catalog migration (slice #2/#7) creating the products table (barcode, name, price, stock fields) and adding a low_stock_threshold column to support low-stock alerts.

nodes.name: af4b5e908f57_shops_gstin_excise_duty_rate_for_8_gst_.py
nodes.summary: GST/excise migration (slice #8) adding a gstin string column and an excise_duty_rate numeric column to shops, laying groundwork for GST/excise invoice-line stub support.

nodes.name: c514c436a0b1_eod_eod_signoffs_business_date_to_date.py
nodes.summary: Follow-up EOD migration altering eod_signoffs.business_date from TIMESTAMP to DATE for correct date-only semantics.

nodes.name: ea0fba754aaa_invoices_invoice_lines_payments_.py
nodes.summary: Checkout & invoicing migration (slice #4) creating the invoices, invoice_lines, and payments tables with status enums, foreign keys to products/lots, and a low_stock_threshold-related index, plus an added column to support billing.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.schemas package (Pydantic schema modules); contains no code.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.security package (auth/authorization helpers); contains no code.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty, zero-content package marker for the app.services package (business-logic service modules).

nodes.name: ONBOARDING.md
nodes.summary: Auto-generated onboarding guide for the Barstock (nexio_stock) project describing project overview, architecture layers, key concepts, a guided tour, a file map by layer, and complexity hotspots.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Tracking document listing the 8 vertical-slice GitHub issues for nexio_stock in dependency order, mapping each to its R-tag requirements and a completion status checklist.

nodes.name: test_auth.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering login for superadmin, owner, receiver, and cashier roles (via username or phone), wrong-password rejection, missing/invalid token 401s, and rejection of cross-scope superadmin tokens on shop endpoints.

nodes.name: test_authorization.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying role-based access control: receiver and cashier get 403 on owner-only staff endpoints, while superadmin can list all staff across shops.

nodes.name: test_health.py
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for the liveness and readiness health-check endpoints, confirming readiness also verifies database connectivity.

nodes.name: test_log_tables.py
nodes.summary: Database-level tests asserting the three audit-log tables (invoicing_logs, stockin_logs, admin_logs) exist and each has the required columns via information_schema queries.

nodes.name: test_products.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the product catalog: owner/role-restricted creation, duplicate barcode 409s, per-bottle-size distinct products, barcode lookup for cashiers, shop-scoped listing, price/threshold updates, and extensive CSV bulk-import scenarios (happy path, per-row errors, low-stock threshold column, missing columns, cashier rejection, UTF-8 BOM handling).

nodes.name: test_staff.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for staff management: owner creates receiver/cashier users, owner cannot create another owner or a superadmin, duplicate username/phone rejected with 409, shop-scoped staff listing, and receiver forbidden from creating staff.

nodes.name: upgrade
nodes.summary: Creates the foundation tables (superadmins, shops, shop_users, invoicing_logs, stockin_logs, admin_logs) with columns, foreign keys, unique constraints, and indexes.

nodes.name: downgrade
nodes.summary: Drops all indexes and tables created by the foundation migration's upgrade path, in reverse order.

nodes.name: upgrade
nodes.summary: Creates the invoices, invoice_lines, and payments tables with status enums, numeric amount columns, foreign keys to products/lots, and supporting indexes for the checkout & invoicing feature.

nodes.name: downgrade
nodes.summary: Drops the payments, invoice_lines, and invoices tables and their indexes, reversing the checkout & invoicing migration.

nodes.name: test_csv_import_per_row_errors_are_surfaced
nodes.summary: Verifies that a CSV bulk-import request with invalid rows returns per-row error details rather than failing the whole import silently.

nodes.name: test_csv_import_with_low_stock_threshold_column
nodes.summary: Verifies CSV bulk-import accepts an optional low_stock_threshold column and applies it to the created products, confirmed via a subsequent lookup.

nodes.name: test_owner_creates_receiver_user
nodes.summary: Verifies an owner can create a receiver-role staff user for their shop via the staff creation endpoint.

nodes.name: test_owner_creates_cashier_user
nodes.summary: Verifies an owner can create a cashier-role staff user for their shop via the staff creation endpoint.

nodes.name: me
nodes.summary: GET /users/me endpoint handler that returns the current authenticated user's public profile, used by the frontend on app boot to route to the correct role screen.

nodes.name: get_logger
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around structlog.get_logger that returns a bound structlog logger for the given module name.

nodes.name: test_cashier_can_request_void_on_signed_off_invoice
nodes.summary: Verifies a cashier can request a void on an invoice that has already been signed off, marking it pending_void rather than voiding it outright.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: FastAPI routers under app/api/ that expose the shop's HTTP surface — auth, staff, shops, products, lots, checkout, voids, EOD dashboard and health — plus the shared error-handling and dependency-injection helpers (_errors.py, deps.py, _test_only.py) that back every endpoint.

layers.name: Schemas (Types) Layer
layers.description: Pydantic request/response models under app/schemas/ that define the wire contract for auth, shops, lots, products, checkout and EOD reconciliation between clients and the API layer.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Business-logic modules under app/services/ that implement checkout pricing, void/reversal workflow, end-of-day reconciliation, low-stock alerting and invoice PDF generation for the single-counter liquor shop.

layers.name: Security & Middleware Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting authentication utilities — JWT issuance/verification and password hashing — used by the API layer's Depends() chain to authorize staff and owner actions.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM models (shops, users, products, lots, invoices, audit logs) together with the Alembic migration chain that evolves the Postgres schema from foundation tables through GST/excise invoicing.

layers.name: Application Entry & Configuration
layers.description: The FastAPI app factory, CLI entry point, DB engine/session setup, logging and pydantic-settings config, alongside project-level config files (pyproject.toml, alembic.ini, .env.example) and the docker-compose service definition that wires the app to Postgres for local/deployed runs.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Pytest suite (with shared conftest fixtures) covering auth/authorization, checkout, voids, EOD reconciliation, low-stock alerts, lots, products, shops, staff, audit log tables and health checks.

layers.name: Documentation & Planning
layers.description: Project README, onboarding guide, and the harness/ planning trail (intake, branch map, ledger, PRD, absences, issues) that records how Barstock's requirements were scoped and delivered feature by feature.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Barstock is a single-counter liquor shop inventory and billing system built for a shop in Odisha, India, on FastAPI + SQLAlchemy 2.0 async + Postgres. The README lays out the stack (uv, Uvicorn, Alembic, structlog, JWT auth via passlib/python-jose) and the app/ layout: main.py, config.py, db.py, models/, schemas/, api/, security/, services/, cli.py. This tour follows that same layout, but traces it through the 8 vertical feature slices the project was actually built in: foundation, products, lots, checkout/invoicing, voids, EOD reconciliation, low-stock alerts, and GST/excise.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: app/main.py is where everything is wired together: it builds the FastAPI app, runs an async lifespan that starts the DB engine and also spins up a background polling task for low-stock alerts, configures middleware, and mounts every router (auth, staff, shops, products, lots, checkout, voids, dashboard, health). It has the highest fan-out of any file in the codebase, touching nearly every router module, which is why it makes the natural starting point for reading the code after the README.

tour.title: Configuration, Database & Logging Foundation
tour.description: Before any feature slice can run, three cross-cutting modules set the ground rules. config.py uses pydantic-settings to load env vars (DB URL, JWT/bcrypt parameters, CORS, low-stock poll interval) behind a cached get_settings(); db.py builds the async SQLAlchemy engine/sessionmaker and exposes the get_db() FastAPI dependency with automatic rollback on error; logging_config.py wires structlog into stdlib logging for structured JSON output to stdout. These three are among the most depended-upon files in the whole project (db.py alone has 12 incoming references), which is why they belong in the 'Application Entry & Configuration' layer rather than any single feature slice.

tour.title: Foundation: Shops, Users & Access Control
tour.description: This is slice #1, foundation, and it establishes the tenancy and role model everything else builds on. app/models/user.py defines the UserRole enum (superadmin/owner/receiver/cashier) and the User ORM model tying credentials to a shop; app/models/shop.py holds shop identity, GSTIN, excise duty rate, and the invoice-numbering counter later slices rely on. app/api/deps.py is the busiest dependency module in the project: it resolves the current user from a bearer JWT and exposes require_role/require_shop_scope guard factories that every other router in this tour uses to enforce who is allowed to do what.

tour.title: Authentication Flow
tour.description: Building on the User and Shop models from Step 4, this group implements the actual login flow. app/security/jwt.py issues and verifies tokens carrying subject, shop_id, role and expiry; app/security/passwords.py wraps bcrypt hashing; app/api/auth.py exposes separate login endpoints for superadmins and shop staff, sharing a private _authenticate helper that checks role, shop scope, active status and password before minting a token; app/schemas/auth.py defines the Pydantic contracts (login requests by username or phone, token responses, staff-creation payloads) that keep the wire format validated end to end.
tour.languageLesson: python-jose + passlib is a common FastAPI pairing: JWTs carry stateless claims (here shop_id and role) that deps.py decodes on every request, avoiding a DB round-trip just to check permissions.

tour.title: Slice #2: Product Catalog
tour.description: This is the second vertical slice delivered: the product catalog. app/models/product.py is the core catalog record (barcode, brand, size, price, low-stock threshold, active flag, shop scoping) referenced by nearly every later feature -- lots, invoices, and low-stock computation all point back to it. app/api/products.py exposes create/list/lookup/update endpoints plus a bulk CSV import endpoint that parses, validates, and upserts many rows per request with per-row error collection, while app/schemas/product.py defines the matching Pydantic contracts, including barcode and size-label validators shared between the single-create and CSV-import paths.

tour.title: Slice #3: Stock Receiving (Lots)
tour.description: Slice #3 introduces lots: the source of truth for how stock enters the shop. app/models/lot.py defines Lot (a single receiving event) and LotLine (per-product quantity received within that lot) -- stock-on-hand is always derived, never stored directly, as received-minus-sold. app/api/lots.py creates lots with per-barcode line validation against the receiving shop and writes a StockinLog audit entry on every create, while app/schemas/lot.py rejects duplicate barcodes within a single lot at the validation layer before it ever reaches the database.

tour.title: Slice #4: Checkout & Invoicing
tour.description: This is the heart of the system: turning a cart into a paid, printable invoice. app/models/invoice.py defines Invoice, InvoiceLine, Payment, EodSignOff and IdempotencyKey along with the InvoiceStatus/PaymentMode enums that drive the later void/reversal state machine. app/services/checkout.py is the core checkout engine -- it resolves cart barcodes to products, computes current stock via the same received-minus-sold subquery pattern as lots, and atomically finalizes a sale under a row lock (FOR UPDATE) with idempotency-key replay protection and payment-split validation. app/api/checkout.py exposes this as an idempotent finalize endpoint plus invoice/PDF retrieval, app/schemas/checkout.py validates that split payments sum correctly, and app/services/invoice_pdf.py renders the finalized invoice as a PDF receipt via ReportLab.
tour.languageLesson: The row-lock-under-transaction pattern (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE inside an async session) is how this codebase prevents two concurrent checkouts from selling the same last unit of stock -- a classic race condition in single-counter POS systems.

tour.title: Slice #5: Voids & Reversal
tour.description: Building directly on the invoice state machine from Step 8, slice #5 handles mistakes and returns. app/services/voids.py implements a two-tier state machine: same-day direct voids apply immediately and restore stock, while post-EOD voids require an owner request/approve/reject cycle where approval creates a signed reversal invoice under the same row-lock pattern used in checkout. app/api/voids.py exposes this as cashier-initiated void/void-request endpoints plus owner approve/reject endpoints, all sharing one audit-log writer so every void action is traceable.

tour.title: Slice #6: EOD Reconciliation & Owner Dashboard
tour.description: Slice #6 closes the daily loop. app/services/eod.py enforces a once-per-business-day sign-off, aggregates a day's invoice and payment totals while excluding voids and reversals, and surfaces sign-off history plus the queue of pending post-EOD void requests that Step 9's voids service creates. app/api/dashboard.py is the owner-facing surface for all of this -- EOD sign-off, EOD totals/history, the pending void queue, and low-stock alerts -- making it the single busiest router in the API layer by fan-out. app/schemas/eod.py defines the response shapes for each of those dashboard views.

tour.title: Slice #7: Low-Stock Alerts & Audit Trail
tour.description: app/services/low_stock.py computes low-stock alerts per shop by deriving current stock the same received-minus-sold way as checkout and lots, via a UNION ALL of two aggregated subqueries, then compares it against each product's effective threshold (a per-product override falling back to the shop default) and returns the most urgent shortages first. app/models/log.py underpins traceability across every slice in this tour: it defines a shared audit-log base and three concrete tables -- InvoicingLog, StockinLog, AdminLog -- that record shop, actor, event type and payload for every billing, stock-receiving, and admin action seen so far.

tour.title: Slice #8: GST/Excise Compliance & Schema Evolution
tour.description: The final delivered slice adds Odisha-specific tax compliance. The GST/excise migration adds a gstin string column and an excise_duty_rate numeric column to the shops table (visible back in Step 4's Shop model), laying the groundwork for GST/excise invoice-line stubs on top of the existing invoicing engine. alembic.ini is the migration runner configuration that every slice's migration -- from the foundation tables in Step 4 through this one -- has been applied through, making it the connective thread across the whole schema's evolution.
tour.languageLesson: Alembic migrations are additive and ordered: each revision file has an upgrade()/downgrade() pair and a pointer to its parent revision, forming a linear chain that lets the schema evolve one vertical slice at a time without ever rewriting history.

tour.title: Deployment, Configuration & the Planning Trail
tour.description: Rounding out the tour, docker-compose.yml defines the local Postgres 16 service (barstock-db) with a pg_isready healthcheck that every developer and the test suite depend on; .env.example documents the env vars a developer must set, including a JWT access-token TTL tuned to a single 12-hour shift; and pyproject.toml declares the FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/Alembic/Pydantic/structlog/passlib dependencies plus the `barstock` CLI entry point and ruff/pytest configuration. Finally, harness/03-prd.md is the frozen Product Requirements Document -- 44 numbered requirements across 14 domains -- that every one of the 8 feature slices in this tour traces back to, giving you the 'why' behind the 'what' you just walked through.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose's depends_on plus a healthcheck (pg_isready) ensures the app container, or the test suite, only starts talking to Postgres once it is actually accepting connections, not just once the process has started.
